19th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief
 
● An explosive drone strike in southern Lebanon severely injured an IDF reserve officer and lightly wounded three reserve NCOs and one NCO.

● Switzerland confirmed the postponement of the US-Iran talks scheduled for June 19-20, citing Vice President Vance’s travel delay amid intensified Israel-Hezbollah fighting, which Tehran has described as a ceasefire violation.

● The Indian LNG carrier DISHA (carrying Qatari LNG) became the first Indian vessel to transit the Strait of Hormuz after the US-Iran peace framework and has safely docked at Dahej Port, Gujarat.

● Japan’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced that a Japanese-flagged vessel, previously detained in the Persian Gulf, safely transited the Strait of Hormuz on June 19.

● Foreign Ministers of Pakistan, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Türkiye will meet in Cairo on June 21 to coordinate positions regarding the Iran-US agreement.

● The Panamanian-flagged container ship Muara transited outbound from Qatar’s Hamad Port through the Strait of Hormuz.

● Iran’s PGSA announced the resumption of commercial vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz.

END OF THE BRIEF

18th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Germany is deploying two ships to the Red Sea as it prepares for a possible military mission in support of security in the Strait of Hormuz, Defense Minister Boris Pistorius announced.

tweets.title:
The biggest challenge for Asim Munir in PoJK may not be the protesters themselves, but the availability of weapons and the potential of that availability of Kalashnikov. 

The more muscular the crackdown, the greater the risk that sections of the population feel alienated and some choose to hit back against the Frontier Corps and Pakistan Army. 

With Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Balochistan already burning, the sustained unrest in PoJK would only add another front to an already overstretched thin security apparatus.

tweets.title:
Many wonder why Rafale’s OSF uses two apertures while Eurofighter’s PIRATE packs most functions into a single IRST turret.

The answer lies in the era they were designed. In the 1980s and early 1990s, air forces placed significant emphasis on long-range visual identification before weapons release. Rafale’s OSF therefore combined a dedicated IRST with a separate high-resolution visual identification channel.

As sensor technology matured, modern IRSTs became capable of extracting far more information from infrared imagery itself, reducing the need for a dedicated visual-ID sensor. That’s why the more integrated PIRATE approach appears more elegant today. Initial units of typhoon did not had PIRATE, it came later.

Different design philosophies shaped by different operational requirements and not necessarily a case of one being better than the other. However OSF forces Rafale to have a smaller radar aperture.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: What the United States Gains ?

Nuclear Commitments: Iran reaffirms it will not develop nuclear weapons and agrees to down-blend its stockpiled enriched material on-site under IAEA supervision.

Reopening the Strait of Hormuz: Iran commits to facilitating the safe, charge-free passage of commercial vessels through the strait for an initial 60 days, with demining completed within 30 days.

Diplomatic Framework: The creation of an executive monitoring mechanism to verify compliance, providing an alternative to active military conflict.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: What Iran Gains ?

Sanctions Relief: The US commits to a schedule for terminating unilateral, UN Security Council, and IAEA sanctions, including issuing waivers to resume Iranian crude oil exports and associated banking services.

Access to Frozen Assets: Restricted Iranian funds will be released for use by Iran's central bank upon implementation.

$300 Billion Reconstruction Plan: The US and regional partners plan to develop a minimum $300 billion economic development package for Iran, to be finalized within 60 days.

Removal of Naval Blockade: The US will lift its naval blockade within 30 days, restoring pre-war shipping traffic, and withdraw military forces from Iran's proximity within 30 days of a final deal.
